_comment: Example tag configurations for the OpenAI Ads Pixel GTM template. Each block represents the values you would enter in the template UI for a given use case. These are illustrative — paste the relevant values into the GTM tag editor; this file is not auto-imported.

1_init_pageview_all_pages.description: Recommended setup for 99% of sites. One tag fires on All Pages, loads the SDK, runs init, and fires page_viewed once per pageview. SPA route changes should fire a separate History Change trigger reusing this same tag (the SDK is deduped automatically).
1_init_pageview_all_pages.trigger: All Pages (Page View) + History Change (for SPAs)

2_purchase_with_ga4_ecommerce_mapping.description: Fires order_created on the purchase confirmation page using GA4's standard `ecommerce` dataLayer object. The template auto-maps transaction_id -> event_id, value -> amount (converted to integer cents), currency, and items[] -> contents[].

3_addtocart_with_manual_fields.description: items_added event with manually specified amount and currency (no ecommerce object on the page). Useful for non-standard cart implementations.

4_lead_form_submission.description: lead_created event for a form submission. Demonstrates the customer_action data shape (no contents[]).

5_custom_event_with_parameters.description: Custom event with arbitrary parameters. Note that custom_event_name goes into eventOptions automatically — do NOT add it as a custom parameter.

6_subscription_with_advanced_matching.description: subscription_created with advanced matching (hashed email + phone + external_id). The template SHA-256-hashes plaintext values automatically; pre-hashed 64-char hex values pass through unchanged.

7_multiple_pixels_init.description: Two separate tags, one per pixel ID, both firing on All Pages. The SDK is loaded once and deduped via templateStorage; each pixel runs its own init.
